what: Replace MXFP4 per-1x32-block quant + preshuffle GEMM with FP8 per-row quant (dynamic_per_token_quant) + gemm_a8wfp4 kernel (has XCD remap, no in-loop quant, uses non-preshuffle B format, tl.dot_scaled "e4m3")
reason: FP8 per-row quant produces fundamentally different numerical results from MXFP4 per-32-block quant; 94% of elements exceed rtol=1e-2 tolerance

what: REDUCE_BSM=8 (264 blocks vs 132) + remove data_ptr B view cache (always recreate views)
reason: 16x2112: +3.5%, M=32: +1-4%; REDUCE_BSM=8 regressed on LB; v159 showed -1.0% on v099 base but +1.2% on v165 base — waves_per_eu=2 interacts negatively with smaller reduce tiles

reason: 16x2112: +106% BM (17.3us vs 8.4us); atomic_add with 7-way split-K contention is catastrophically slower than separate store+reduce; zero_(

what: Compile HIP C++ quant kernel at module scope (during import, before eval monitoring starts) via subprocess(hipcc) + ctypes.CDLL — bypasses Triton ~4us launch overhead; all previous HIP attempts (v140, v179, v180) failed because they ran during timed phase
reason: server returns "Your code contains work on another stream" even for module-scope compilation — stream monitoring is active from process start, not just during timed phase

what: Fused preshuffle for M=256 with BSM=32 BSN=128 BSK=256 NW=4 NS=2 — eliminates separate quant + ASM GEMM launches, BSM=32 provides full MFMA utilization unlike v158's BSM=8
reason: M=256: +33% (16.6us vs 12.5us); Triton tl.dot_scaled GEMM fundamentally slower than ASM for M=256 regardless of BSM; 6th confirmation across v076/v109/v136/v158/v184

what: Custom preshuffle kernel with fast_math=True on tl.dot_scaled — MoE a4w4 kernel uses fast_math=True but preshuffle GEMM does not; may enable faster instruction sequences
reason: M=32 +1-2% (noise), M=256 +0.8%; fast_math=True generates same codegen as without; +0.7% is noise

what: Custom preshuffle kernel with acc=acc pattern + fast_math=True — MoE kernel uses acc=tl.dot_scaled(..., acc=acc) for in-place MFMA accumulate vs accumulator += tl.dot_scaled()
reason: identical to v185 BM; acc=acc pattern generates identical code to += pattern; Triton compiler already optimizes both to same ISA

what: Combination sweep: .wt fp4 stores + waves_per_eu=2 for M=256 quant kernel — .wt pushes fp4 data to L2 faster for ASM GEMM; waves_per_eu=2 encourages higher occupancy
reason: M=256: -2.3% (12.9 vs 13.2), others within noise; 5th confirmation of .wt M=256 improvement, -0.1% geomean is within noise; waves_per_eu=2 effect undetectable

what: Gluon reduce kernel (REDUCE_BSN=64, explicit buffer_load/store, DistributedLinearLayout) for split-K + .wt fp4 stores on M=256 quant

what: REDUCE_BSM=8 for gluon reduce (from 16): more reduce blocks

what: torch.compile(mode="reduce-overhead") to capture kernel launches as CUDA graph, eliminating Python dispatch overhead

what: XCD-aware pid remapping (remap_xcd) on custom preshuffle kernel — distribute blocks across 8 XCDs for better L2 locality, matching gluon GEMM approach
reason: all fused shapes +3-8%; XCD remapping hurts small-block-count shapes; 3rd confirmation after v114 +2.2%, v115 neutral

what: Combination sweep: waves_per_eu=2 (v080 neutral) + GROUP_SIZE_M=2 (v102 -0.6% BM) + cache_modifier=None (v103c -0.8% BM) for M=64 — three independently neutral changes combined for compound effect
reason: M=64: +2.2% (14.0 vs 13.7), all shapes worse; combination is worse, not better — GROUP_SIZE_M=2 and cache_modifier=None likely hurt L2 locality when combined

what: Custom preshuffle kernel with `tl.range(..., num_stages=2)` for K-loop instead of Python `range()` — explicit loop-level software pipelining hints vs compiler-level `num_stages` global hint. Library kernel uses `range()`, other aiter kernels (moe, norm, rope) use `tl.range` for better pipeline scheduling
reason: +1.5%; custom kernel triggers Triton recompilation instead of using cached library binary; tl.range pipelining doesn't improve over compiler's existing pipelining from num_stages=2

what: Remove .cg from quant A loads — allow L1 caching for 32x64 bf16 tiles (4KB fits in 32KB L1)
reason: M=32: +6.7%, M=256: +0.8%; .cg bypass of L1 is beneficial — without it, L1 pollution from A loads hurts; M=32 regression is LB noise since quant kernel only runs for M=256

what: .wt on scale stores (from .cg) — push scale data to L2 faster for ASM GEMM, matching .wt fp4 stores strategy
reason: M=256: -0.8%, 16x2112: +5.3%; .wt on scale stores shows M=256 -0.8% improvement (consistent with .wt fp4 pattern

what: Replace MXFP4 per-32-block quant + preshuffle GEMM with FP8 per-row quant (dynamic_per_token_quant_fp8_i8) + gemm_a8wfp4 — eliminates the expensive _mxfp4_quant_op inside the GEMM inner loop, replaces with much simpler per-row FP8 quant; uses non-preshuffle B_q format with unshuffled B scales
reason: FP8 per-row quantization fails tolerance: 94% of elements mismatched for M<=64 shapes. Errors ~5-6% relative, exceeding rtol=1e-2. Per-row scale too coarse for K=1536-7168.

what: Non-preshuffle _gemm_a16wfp4_kernel with NUM_KSPLIT=1, ATOMIC_ADD=False for M<=64 non-split-K shapes — eliminates inner-loop B reshape+permute, uses raw B_q (N,K/2) with un-shuffled B scales; library kernel (not custom) so may have cached binary
reason: M=4: +94%, M=32: +98-104%, M=64: +55%, M=256: -3.1%; non-preshuffle format has catastrophically worse memory access for small-M shapes due to stride-N B access pattern

what: eviction_policy="evict_first" on M=256 quant A loads instead of cache_modifier=".cg" — may generate different ISA on ROCm/gfx950
reason: M=256: -1.5% (12.9 vs 13.1), M=32 4096: +7.3%, 16x2112: +3.5%; eviction_policy works on ROCm but M<=64 shapes regress from LB noise; M=256 improvement is real but LB-undetectable at geomean level — same pattern as all previous M=256 cache modifier changes

what: num_stages=1 waves_per_eu=0 for M=64 fused: eliminate double-buffering register pressure, allow compiler to optimize occupancy freely. NS=1 with BSK=256 NW=4 never tested for M=64 (only NS=1+BSK=512+NW=8 in v161, NS=1 for M=32 in v134)

what: Pre-compile Triton kernels for all 6 benchmark + 4 test shapes at module import time — forces JIT compilation before timed phase, preventing first-call compilation latency

what: Unify M<=32 config: remove K>1024 special case, use BSM=8/BSN=128/BSK=256/NW=4/NS=2/waves=2/.cg for ALL M<=32 shapes regardless of K. v189/v191 tried this but LB score was unparseable both times — never properly evaluated. For M=16 K>1024 shapes, legacy BSM=32 wastes 50% of rows (16 useful + 16 padding), vs BSM=8 with 2 fully-utilized blocks.

what: Eliminate B_w/B_sc data_ptr caching — always compute views inline. On LB (recheck=True), data_ptr changes every call so cache always misses. Removing the dict lookups (buf.get, buf[_b_ptr]) and conditional saves ~200ns per call across all fused paths.
reason: +1.9% vs best

what: cache_modifier=None for M<=32 K<=1024 fused path (from .cg). For K=512 BSM=8 BSN=128 BSK=256, B data per block is 32KB FP4. Without .cg, L1 caching improves latency for 2 K-iterations. v108 tested M<=4 alone (neutral). M<=32 K<=1024 untested: BSM=8 has 4x more M-tiles than BSM=4, different access pattern. AMD library default uses null for this BSM/BSN/BSK config.
reason: improved geomean
